financial.note: The Ongkosit family, led by Pitharn Ongkosit (~13.94%), maintains ~33% ownership; their strategic focus on 'New S-Curve' automation directly dictates capital expenditure cycles.
Provides long-term credit facilities for the Lat Krabang and Ayutthaya plant upgrades; interest expense is sensitive to Thai policy rate shifts.
Holds ~6.32% stake; fluctuates based on foreign fund flow (FOMO/FUD) regarding Thai electronics exports.

supply_chain.note: Automotive segment accounts for ~70% of revenue. KCE is a Tier-1/Tier-2 supplier for high-density interconnect (HDI) PCBs used in ADAS and EV control systems.
Copper is a core cost component. Every $1,000/ton increase in copper prices typically results in a ~1% gross margin contraction with a 3-month lag.
KCE's internal production of Prepreg and Copper Clad Laminate (CCL) provides a vertical hedge against global supply chain bottlenecks.

equity_cross_holdings.note: Absorbed Chemtronic Technology via amalgamation in Jan 2026 to streamline the chemical recycling supply chain.
Established to manage international acquisitions, including the 100% stake in International Circuits Limited (UK).

competitors.note: Competes for talent and investor capital in the Thai electronics sector, though DELTA focuses more on power supplies than pure PCB.
Direct rival in the automotive EMS and IC packaging space; often trades in a high correlation with KCE.
Competes in industrial-grade PCB assembly and global export markets.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: As a 100% export company, THB depreciation acts as a direct tailwind for revenue translation and competitiveness.
The transition from ICE to EV increases PCB content per vehicle by 3-5x, serving as KCE’s primary long-term growth driver.
PCB manufacturing is energy-intensive; rising utility costs for massive lamination and plating lines directly compress EBITDA.
